medications.drug_name: intensive greasy emollient (e.g., 50/50 white soft paraffin/liquid paraffin)
medications.indication_rationale: Liberal frequent emollient softens rigid plaques and stops painful fissuring — the mainstay of symptom relief.
medications.dose_route_frequency: apply generously to the whole body several times daily, especially after showering

medications.indication_rationale: To the most inflamed/fissured plaques; step down as it settles.
medications.dose_route_frequency: apply twice daily to worst plaques (as per source note)

medications.drug_name: topical therapy alone as definitive management of this severe flare
medications.indication_rationale: Topicals alone are insufficient for a flare of this magnitude — urgent dermatology referral for systemic therapy is needed.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Apply emollient immediately after showering onto damp skin, don't pick/peel the scales (prevents Auspitz bleeding and Koebner), and wear loose soft cotton clothing.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Restores flexibility, reduces fissuring, and avoids triggering new plaques.

patient_education_points.point: Your psoriasis has flared badly, triggered by the throat infection and stress. The most important immediate treatment is heavy, frequent greasy moisturiser to soften the plaques and stop the painful cracking, plus the steroid ointment on the worst areas.
A flare this severe usually needs specialist (systemic) treatment, so an urgent dermatology referral is being arranged. Don't pick the scales.
If your whole body turns bright red, you get a high fever with shivering or feel unable to keep warm, or the cracks leak thick yellow pus — go to the emergency department immediately.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Flare/fissuring, secondary infection, and that the dermatology referral is actioned.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Whole-body erythema, high fever/rigors/inability to keep warm, or purulent infection — emergency care.

must_include_items: Recognise a severe plaque psoriasis flare and screen for erythrodermic/pustular variants and secondary sepsis
Intensive emollients + potent topical corticosteroid + analgesia
Urgent dermatology referral for systemic therapy (topicals alone insufficient)
acceptable_alternatives: Coal tar / vitamin D analogue combinations via dermatology
errors_of_commission: Topical steroid alone as definitive management
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires emollient-led symptom control + potent topical + urgent dermatology referral, with an erythroderma/pustular/sepsis safety-net.
